Name: model_tag
Description:
The model tag to serve (optional if specified in
config)

Name: aggregate-engine-logging
Description:
Log aggregate rather than per-engine statistics when
using data parallelism.

Name: api-server-count
Description:
How many API server processes to run. Defaults to
data_parallel_size if not specified.

Name: config
Description:
Read CLI options from a config file. Must be a YAML
with the following options: https://docs.vllm.ai/en/la
test/configuration/serve_args.html

Name: disable-log-stats
Description: Disable logging statistics.

Name: enable-log-requests
Description:
Enable logging request information, dependent on log
level:
- INFO: Request ID, parameters and LoRA request.
- DEBUG: Prompt inputs (e.g: text, token IDs). You can
set the minimum log level via `VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL`.

Name: no-enable-log-requests
Description:
Enable logging request information, dependent on log
level:
- INFO: Request ID, parameters and LoRA request.
- DEBUG: Prompt inputs (e.g: text, token IDs). You can
set the minimum log level via `VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL`.

Name: fail-on-environ-validation
Description:
If set, the engine will raise an error if environment
validation fails.

Name: no-fail-on-environ-validation
Description:
If set, the engine will raise an error if environment
validation fails.

Name: gdn-prefill-backend
Description: Select GDN prefill backend.

Name: grpc
Description:
Launch a gRPC server instead of the HTTP OpenAI-
compatible server. Requires: pip install vllm[grpc].

Name: headless
Description:
Run in headless mode. See multi-node data parallel
documentation for more details.

Name: shutdown-timeout
Description: Shutdown timeout in seconds. 0 = abort, >0 = wait.

Name: h, --help
Description: show this help message and exit

Name: allow-credentials
Description: Allow credentials.

Name: no-allow-credentials
Description: Allow credentials.

Name: allowed-headers
Description: Allowed headers.

Name: allowed-methods
Description: Allowed methods.

Name: allowed-origins
Description: Allowed origins.

Name: api-key
Description:
If provided, the server will require one of these keys
to be presented in the header.

Name: chat-template
Description: N/A

Name: chat-template-content-format
Description: N/A

Name: data-parallel-supervisor-port
Description:
HTTP port for aggregated health endpoints in multi-
port external LB mode.

Name: default-chat-template-kwargs
Description:
Should either be a valid JSON string or JSON keys
passed individually.

Name: disable-access-log-for-endpoints
Description:
Comma-separated list of endpoint paths to exclude from
uvicorn access logs. This is useful to reduce log
noise from high-frequency endpoints like health
checks. Example: "/health,/metrics,/ping". When set,
access logs for requests to these paths will be
suppressed while keeping logs for other endpoints.

Name: disable-fastapi-docs
Description:
Disable FastAPI's OpenAPI schema, Swagger UI, and
ReDoc endpoint.

Name: no-disable-fastapi-docs
Description:
Disable FastAPI's OpenAPI schema, Swagger UI, and
ReDoc endpoint.

Name: disable-uvicorn-access-log
Description: Disable uvicorn access log.

Name: no-disable-uvicorn-access-log
Description: Disable uvicorn access log.

Name: dp-supervisor-probe-failure-threshold
Description:
Number of consecutive connection-error retries before
a child health probe is declared failed in multi-port
external LB mode.

Name: dp-supervisor-probe-interval-s
Description:
Seconds between aggregated health probes in multi-port
external LB mode.

Name: dp-supervisor-probe-timeout-s
Description:
Seconds to wait between retries when a child health
probe fails with a connection error in multi-port
external LB mode.

Name: enable-auto-tool-choice
Description: N/A

Name: no-enable-auto-tool-choice
Description: N/A

Name: enable-flash-late-interaction
Description:
If set, run pooling score MaxSim on GPU in the API
server process. Can significantly improve late-
interaction scoring performance.

Name: no-enable-flash-late-interaction
Description:
If set, run pooling score MaxSim on GPU in the API
server process. Can significantly improve late-
interaction scoring performance.

Name: enable-force-include-usage
Description: N/A

Name: no-enable-force-include-usage
Description: N/A

Name: enable-log-deltas
Description: N/A

Name: no-enable-log-deltas
Description: N/A

Name: enable-log-outputs
Description: N/A

Name: no-enable-log-outputs
Description: N/A

Name: enable-offline-docs
Description:
Enable offline FastAPI documentation for air-gapped
environments. Uses vendored static assets bundled with
vLLM.

Name: no-enable-offline-docs
Description:
Enable offline FastAPI documentation for air-gapped
environments. Uses vendored static assets bundled with
vLLM.

Name: enable-prompt-tokens-details
Description: N/A

Name: no-enable-prompt-tokens-details
Description: N/A

Name: enable-request-id-headers
Description:
If specified, API server will add X-Request-Id header
to responses.

Name: no-enable-request-id-headers
Description:
If specified, API server will add X-Request-Id header
to responses.

Name: enable-server-load-tracking
Description: N/A

Name: no-enable-server-load-tracking
Description: N/A

Name: enable-ssl-refresh
Description: Refresh SSL Context when SSL certificate files change

Name: no-enable-ssl-refresh
Description: Refresh SSL Context when SSL certificate files change

Name: enable-tokenizer-info-endpoint
Description: N/A

Name: no-enable-tokenizer-info-endpoint
Description: N/A

Name: exclude-tools-when-tool-choice-none
Description: N/A

Name: no-exclude-tools-when-tool-choice-none
Description: N/A

Name: fingerprint-mode
Description: N/A

Name: fingerprint-value
Description: N/A

Name: h11-max-header-count
Description:
Maximum number of HTTP headers allowed in a request
for h11 parser. Helps mitigate header abuse. Default:
256.

Name: h11-max-incomplete-event-size
Description:
Maximum size (bytes) of an incomplete HTTP event
(header or body) for h11 parser. Helps mitigate header
abuse. Default: 4194304 (4 MB).

Name: host
Description: Host name.

Name: log-config-file
Description: N/A

Name: log-error-stack
Description: N/A

Name: no-log-error-stack
Description: N/A

Name: lora-modules
Description: N/A

Name: max-log-len
Description: N/A

Name: middleware
Description:
Additional ASGI middleware to apply to the app. We
accept multiple
--middleware arguments. The value should be an import
path. If a function is provided, vLLM will add it to
the server using `@app.middleware('http')`. If a class
is provided, vLLM will add it to the server using
`app.add_middleware()`.

Name: port
Description: Port number.

Name: response-role
Description: N/A

Name: return-tokens-as-token-ids
Description: N/A

Name: no-return-tokens-as-token-ids
Description: N/A

Name: root-path
Description:
FastAPI root_path when app is behind a path based
routing proxy.

Name: ssl-ca-certs
Description: The CA certificates file.

Name: ssl-cert-reqs
Description:
Whether client certificate is required (see stdlib ssl
module's).

Name: ssl-certfile
Description: The file path to the SSL cert file.

Name: ssl-ciphers
Description:
SSL cipher suites for HTTPS (TLS 1.2 and below only).
Example: 'ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-
CHACHA20-POLY1305'

Name: ssl-keyfile
Description: The file path to the SSL key file.

Name: tokens-only
Description: N/A

Name: no-tokens-only
Description: N/A

Name: tool-call-parser or name registered in --tool-parser-plugin
Description: N/A

Name: tool-parser-plugin
Description: N/A

Name: tool-server
Description: N/A

Name: trust-request-chat-template
Description: N/A

Name: no-trust-request-chat-template
Description: N/A

Name: uds
Description:
Unix domain socket path. If set, host and port
arguments are ignored.

Name: uvicorn-log-level
Description: Log level for uvicorn.

Name: allow-deprecated-quantization
Description: Whether to allow deprecated quantization methods.

Name: no-allow-deprecated-quantization
Description: Whether to allow deprecated quantization methods.

Name: allowed-local-media-path
Description:
Allowing API requests to read local images or videos
from directories specified by the server file system.
This is a security risk. Should only be enabled in
trusted environments.

Name: allowed-media-domains
Description:
If set, only media URLs that belong to this domain can
be used for multi-modal inputs.

Name: code-revision
Description:
The specific revision to use for the model code on the
Hugging Face Hub. It can be a branch name, a tag name,
or a commit id. If unspecified, will use the default
version.

Name: config-format
Description:
The format of the model config to load:
- "auto" will try to load the config in hf format if
available after trying to load in mistral format.
- "hf" will load the config in hf format.
- "mistral" will load the config in mistral format.

Name: convert
Description:
Convert the model using adapters defined in
[vllm.model_executor.models.adapters][]. The most
common use case is to adapt a text generation model to
be used for pooling tasks.

Name: disable-cascade-attn
Description:
Disable cascade attention for V1. While cascade
attention does not change the mathematical
correctness, disabling it could be useful for
preventing potential numerical issues. This defaults
to True, so users must opt in to cascade attention by
setting this to False. Even when this is set to False,
cascade attention will only be used when the heuristic
tells that it's beneficial.

Name: no-disable-cascade-attn
Description:
Disable cascade attention for V1. While cascade
attention does not change the mathematical
correctness, disabling it could be useful for
preventing potential numerical issues. This defaults
to True, so users must opt in to cascade attention by
setting this to False. Even when this is set to False,
cascade attention will only be used when the heuristic
tells that it's beneficial.

Name: disable-sliding-window
Description:
Whether to disable sliding window. If True, we will
disable the sliding window functionality of the model,
capping to sliding window size. If the model does not
support sliding window, this argument is ignored.

Name: no-disable-sliding-window
Description:
Whether to disable sliding window. If True, we will
disable the sliding window functionality of the model,
capping to sliding window size. If the model does not
support sliding window, this argument is ignored.

Name: dtype
Description:
Data type for model weights and activations:
- "auto" will use FP16 precision for FP32 and FP16
models, and BF16 precision for BF16 models.
- "half" for FP16. Recommended for AWQ quantization.
- "float16" is the same as "half".
- "bfloat16" for a balance between precision and
range.
- "float" is shorthand for FP32 precision.
- "float32" for FP32 precision.

Name: enable-cumem-allocator
Description:
Enable the custom cumem allocator to leverage advanced
GPU memory allocation features such as multi-node
NVLink support.
Sleep mode automatically enables this allocator. Only
cuda and hip platforms are supported.

Name: no-enable-cumem-allocator
Description:
Enable the custom cumem allocator to leverage advanced
GPU memory allocation features such as multi-node
NVLink support.
Sleep mode automatically enables this allocator. Only
cuda and hip platforms are supported.

Name: enable-prompt-embeds
Description:
If `True`, enables passing text embeddings as inputs
via the `prompt_embeds` key.
WARNING: The vLLM engine may crash if incorrect shape
of embeddings is passed. Only enable this flag for
trusted users!

Name: no-enable-prompt-embeds
Description:
If `True`, enables passing text embeddings as inputs
via the `prompt_embeds` key.
WARNING: The vLLM engine may crash if incorrect shape
of embeddings is passed. Only enable this flag for
trusted users!

Name: enable-return-routed-experts
Description: Whether to return routed experts.

Name: no-enable-return-routed-experts
Description: Whether to return routed experts.

Name: enable-sleep-mode
Description:
Enable sleep mode for the engine (only cuda and hip
platforms are supported).

Name: no-enable-sleep-mode
Description:
Enable sleep mode for the engine (only cuda and hip
platforms are supported).

Name: enforce-eager
Description:
Whether to always use eager-mode PyTorch. If True, we
will disable CUDA graph and always execute the model
in eager mode. If False, we will use CUDA graph and
eager execution in hybrid for maximal performance and
flexibility.

Name: no-enforce-eager
Description:
Whether to always use eager-mode PyTorch. If True, we
will disable CUDA graph and always execute the model
in eager mode. If False, we will use CUDA graph and
eager execution in hybrid for maximal performance and
flexibility.

Name: generation-config
Description:
The folder path to the generation config. Defaults to
`"auto"`, the generation config will be loaded from
model path. If set to `"vllm"`, no generation config
is loaded, vLLM defaults will be used. If set to a
folder path, the generation config will be loaded from
the specified folder path. If `max_new_tokens` is
specified in generation config, then it sets a server-
wide limit on the number of output tokens for all
requests.

Name: hf-config-path
Description:
Name or path of the Hugging Face config to use. If
unspecified, model name or path will be used.

Name: hf-overrides
Description:
If a dictionary, contains arguments to be forwarded to
the Hugging Face config. If a callable, it is called
to update the HuggingFace config.

Name: hf-token
Description:
The token to use as HTTP bearer authorization for
remote files . If `True`, will use the token generated
when running `hf auth login` (stored in
`~/.cache/huggingface/token`).

Name: io-processor-plugin
Description: IOProcessor plugin name to load at model startup

Name: logits-processors
Description:
One or more logits processors' fully-qualified class
names or class definitions

Name: logprobs-mode
Description:
Indicates the content returned in the logprobs and
prompt_logprobs. Supported mode: 1) raw_logprobs, 2)
processed_logprobs, 3) raw_logits, 4)
processed_logits. Raw means the values before applying
any logit processors, like bad words. Processed means
the values after applying all processors, including
temperature and top_k/top_p.

Name: max-logprobs
Description:
Maximum number of log probabilities to return when
`logprobs` is specified in `SamplingParams`. The
default value comes the default for the OpenAI Chat
Completions API. -1 means no cap, i.e. all
(output_length * vocab_size) logprobs are allowed to
be returned and it may cause OOM.

Name: max-model-len
Description:
Model context length (prompt and output). If
unspecified, will be automatically derived from the
model config.
When passing via `--max-model-len`, supports
k/m/g/K/M/G in human-readable format. Examples:
- 1k -> 1000
- 1K -> 1024
- 25.6k -> 25,600
- -1 or 'auto' -> Automatically choose the maximum
model length that fits in GPU memory. This will use
the model's maximum context length if it fits,
otherwise it will find the largest length that can be
accommodated.
Parse human-readable integers like '1k', '2M', etc.
Including decimal values with decimal multipliers.
Also accepts -1 or 'auto' as a special value for auto-
detection.
Examples:
- '1k' -> 1,000
- '1K' -> 1,024
- '25.6k' -> 25,600
- '-1' or 'auto' -> -1 (special value for auto-
detection)

Name: model
Description:
Name or path of the Hugging Face model to use. It is
also used as the content for `model_name` tag in
metrics output when `served_model_name` is not
specified.

Name: model-impl
Description:
Which implementation of the model to use:
- "auto" will try to use the vLLM implementation, if
it exists, and fall back to the Transformers
implementation if no vLLM implementation is available.
- "vllm" will use the vLLM model implementation.
- "transformers" will use the Transformers model
implementation.
- "terratorch" will use the TerraTorch model
implementation.

Name: override-attention-dtype
Description: Override dtype for attention

Name: override-generation-config
Description:
Overrides or sets generation config. e.g.
`{"temperature": 0.5}`. If used with `--generation-
config auto`, the override parameters will be merged
with the default config from the model. If used with
`--generation-config vllm`, only the override
parameters are used.
Should either be a valid JSON string or JSON keys
passed individually.

Name: pooler-config
Description:
Pooler config which controls the behaviour of output
pooling in pooling models.
API docs: https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/api/vllm/conf
ig/#vllm.config.PoolerConfig
Should either be a valid JSON string or JSON keys
passed individually.

Name: quantization
Description:
Method used to quantize the weights. If `None`, we
first check the `quantization_config` attribute in the
model config file. If that is `None`, we assume the
model weights are not quantized and use `dtype` to
determine the data type of the weights.

Name: quantization-config
Description:
User-facing quantization configuration. Carries per-
layer-kind specs (linear, moe) and ignore patterns;
see :class:`QuantizationConfigArgs`. Auto-populated
from the matching online shorthand when `quantization`
is one of the values in
`ONLINE_QUANT_SHORTHAND_NAMES`.
API docs: https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/api/vllm/conf
ig/#vllm.config.QuantizationConfigArgs
Should either be a valid JSON string or JSON keys
passed individually.

Name: renderer-num-workers
Description:
Number of worker threads in the renderer thread pool.
The pool is consumed by the async renderer path (e.g.
the OpenAI-compatible API server started by `vllm
serve`) to parallelize tokenization, chat template
rendering, and multimodal preprocessing across
concurrent requests.
The offline `LLM` entrypoint uses the synchronous
renderer path and processes prompts (including
multimodal preprocessing) serially, so this setting
has no effect there.

Name: revision
Description:
The specific model version to use. It can be a branch
name, a tag name, or a commit id. If unspecified, will
use the default version.

Name: runner
Description:
The type of model runner to use. Each vLLM instance
only supports one model runner, even if the same model
can be used for multiple types.

Name: seed
Description:
Random seed for reproducibility.
We must set the global seed because otherwise,
different tensor parallel workers would sample
different tokens, leading to inconsistent results.

Name: served-model-name
Description:
The model name(s) used in the API. If multiple names
are provided, the server will respond to any of the
provided names. The model name in the model field of a
response will be the first name in this list. If not
specified, the model name will be the same as the
`--model` argument. Noted that this name(s) will also
be used in `model_name` tag content of prometheus
metrics, if multiple names provided, metrics tag will
take the first one.

Name: skip-tokenizer-init
Description:
Skip initialization of tokenizer and detokenizer.
Expects valid `prompt_token_ids` and `None` for prompt
from the input. The generated output will contain
token ids.

Name: no-skip-tokenizer-init
Description:
Skip initialization of tokenizer and detokenizer.
Expects valid `prompt_token_ids` and `None` for prompt
from the input. The generated output will contain
token ids.

Name: tokenizer
Description:
Name or path of the Hugging Face tokenizer to use. If
unspecified, model name or path will be used.

Name: tokenizer-mode
Description:
Tokenizer mode:
- "auto" will use the tokenizer from `mistral_common`
for Mistral models if available, otherwise it will use
the "hf" tokenizer.
- "hf" will use the fast tokenizer if available.
- "slow" will always use the slow tokenizer.
- "mistral" will always use the tokenizer from
`mistral_common`.
- "deepseek_v32" will always use the tokenizer from
`deepseek_v32`.
- "deepseek_v4" will always use the tokenizer from
`deepseek_v4`.
- "qwen_vl" will always use the tokenizer from
`qwen_vl`.
- Other custom values can be supported via plugins.
To swap the Rust BPE backend that powers HF fast
tokenizers for the
[fastokens](https://github.com/crusoecloud/fastokens)
implementation, set `VLLM_USE_FASTOKENS=1` instead —
that override applies to any mode that loads an HF
fast tokenizer (`hf`, `deepseek_v32`, `deepseek_v4`,
`qwen_vl`, …).

Name: tokenizer-revision
Description:
The specific revision to use for the tokenizer on the
Hugging Face Hub. It can be a branch name, a tag name,
or a commit id. If unspecified, will use the default
version.

Name: trust-remote-code
Description:
Trust remote code (e.g., from HuggingFace) when
downloading the model and tokenizer.

Name: no-trust-remote-code
Description:
Trust remote code (e.g., from HuggingFace) when
downloading the model and tokenizer.

Name: use-fp64-gumbel
Description:
Whether to use FP64 (instead of FP32) for the Gumbel
noise used by the sampler. FP64 reduces the chance of
ties in Gumbel-max sampling at the cost of
significantly lower kernel throughput on most GPUs.

Name: no-use-fp64-gumbel
Description:
Whether to use FP64 (instead of FP32) for the Gumbel
noise used by the sampler. FP64 reduces the chance of
ties in Gumbel-max sampling at the cost of
significantly lower kernel throughput on most GPUs.

Name: task
Description: The task used for pooling.

Name: pooling_type
Description: The pooling method used for pooling.

Name: seq_pooling_type
Description: The pooling method used for sequence pooling.

Name: tok_pooling_type
Description: The pooling method used for tokenwise pooling.

Name: use_activation
Description: Whether to apply activation function to the pooler outputs.

Name: dimensions
Description: Reduce the dimensions of embeddings if model supports matryoshka representation.

Name: enable_chunked_processing
Description: Whether to enable chunked processing for long inputs.

Name: max_embed_len
Description: Maximum input length allowed for embedding generation.

Name: logit_mean
Description: If provided, subtract this value from classification logits before activation.

Name: logit_sigma
Description: If provided, divide the classification logits by this value after mean subtraction.

Name: step_tag_id
Description: If set, only the score corresponding to the step_tag_id in the generated sentence should be returned.

Name: returned_token_ids
Description: A list of indices for the vocabulary dimensions to be extracted.

Name: linear
Description: Quantization spec applied to LinearBase layers.

Name: moe
Description: Quantization spec applied to FusedMoE layers.

Name: ignore
Description: Layers to skip quantization for.

Name: download-dir
Description:
Directory to download and load the weights, default to
the default cache directory of Hugging Face.

Name: ignore-patterns
Description:
The list of patterns to ignore when loading the model.
Default to "original/**/*" to avoid repeated loading
of llama's checkpoints.

Name: load-format
Description:
The format of the model weights to load.
- "auto" will try to load the weights in the
safetensors format and fall back to the pytorch bin
format if safetensors format is not available.
- "pt" will load the weights in the pytorch bin
format.
- "safetensors" will load the weights in the
safetensors format.
- "instanttensor" will load the Safetensors weights on
CUDA devices using InstantTensor, which enables
distributed loading with pipelined prefetching and
fast direct I/O.
- "npcache" will load the weights in pytorch format
and store a numpy cache to speed up the loading.
- "dummy" will initialize the weights with random
values, which is mainly for profiling.
- "tensorizer" will use CoreWeave's tensorizer library
for fast weight loading. See the Tensorize vLLM Model
script in the Examples section for more information.
- "runai_streamer" will load the Safetensors weights
using Run:ai Model Streamer.
- "runai_streamer_sharded" will load weights from pre-
sharded checkpoint files using Run:ai Model Streamer.
- "bitsandbytes" will load the weights using
bitsandbytes quantization.
- "sharded_state" will load weights from pre-sharded
checkpoint files, supporting efficient loading of
tensor-parallel models.
- "gguf" will load weights from GGUF format files
(details specified in https://github.com/ggml-
org/ggml/blob/master/docs/gguf.md).
- "mistral" will load weights from consolidated
safetensors files used by Mistral models.
- "modelexpress" will load weights using ModelExpress.
- Other custom values can be supported via plugins.

Name: model-loader-extra-config
Description:
Extra config for model loader. This will be passed to
the model loader corresponding to the chosen
load_format.

Name: pt-load-map-location
Description:
The map location for loading pytorch checkpoint, to
support loading checkpoints can only be loaded on
certain devices like "cuda", this is equivalent to
`{"": "cuda"}`. Another supported format is mapping
from different devices like from GPU 1 to GPU 0:
`{"cuda:1": "cuda:0"}`. Note that when passed from
command line, the strings in dictionary need to be
double quoted for json parsing. For more details, see
the original doc for `map_location` parameter in
[`torch.load`][] parameter.

Name: safetensors-load-strategy
Description:
Specifies the loading strategy for safetensors
weights.
- None (default): Uses memory-mapped (lazy) loading.
When an NFS filesystem is detected and the total
checkpoint size fits within 90%% of available RAM,
prefetching is enabled automatically.
- "lazy": Weights are memory-mapped from the file.
This enables on-demand loading and is highly efficient
for models on local storage. Unlike the default
(None), auto-prefetch on NFS is not performed.
- "eager": The entire file is read into CPU memory
upfront before loading. This is recommended for models
on network filesystems (e.g., Lustre, NFS) as it
avoids inefficient random reads, significantly
speeding up model initialization. However, it uses
more CPU RAM.
- "prefetch": Checkpoint files are read into the OS
page cache before workers load them, speeding up the
model loading phase. Useful on network or high-latency
storage.
- "torchao": Weights are loaded in upfront and then
reconstructed into torchao tensor subclasses. This is
used when the checkpoint was quantized using torchao
and saved using safetensors. Needs `torchao >=
0.14.0`.

Name: safetensors-prefetch-block-size
Description:
Read size in bytes for each safetensors checkpoint
file prefetch.
Parse human-readable integers like '1k', '2M', etc.
Including decimal values with decimal multipliers.
Examples:
- '1k' -> 1,000
- '1K' -> 1,024
- '25.6k' -> 25,600

Name: safetensors-prefetch-num-threads
Description:
Number of worker threads used to prefetch safetensors
checkpoint files into the OS page cache when
safetensors prefetching is enabled.

Name: use-tqdm-on-load
Description:
Whether to enable tqdm for showing progress bar when
loading model weights.

Name: no-use-tqdm-on-load
Description:
Whether to enable tqdm for showing progress bar when
loading model weights.

Name: attention-backend
Description:
Attention backend to use. Use "auto" or None for
automatic selection.

Name: backend
Description: The backend to use for weight transfer during RL training.

Name: flash_attn_version
Description: Force vllm to use a specific flash-attention version (2, 3, or 4).

Name: use_prefill_decode_attention
Description: Use separate prefill and decode kernels for attention.

Name: flash_attn_max_num_splits_for_cuda_graph
Description: Flash Attention max number of splits for CUDA graph decode.

Name: tq_max_kv_splits_for_cuda_graph
Description: TurboQuant max NUM_KV_SPLITS for CUDA graph decode.

Name: use_trtllm_attention
Description: If set to True/False, use or don't use the TRTLLM attention backend in flashinfer.

Name: disable_flashinfer_q_quantization
Description: If set, when using fp8 kv, do not quantize Q to fp8.

Name: mla_prefill_backend
Description: MLA prefill backend to use.

Name: use_prefill_query_quantization
Description: If set, quantize query for attention in prefill.

Name: use_fp4_indexer_cache
Description: If set, use fp4 indexer cache for DeepSeek V3.2 family models.

Name: use_non_causal
Description: Whether to use non-causal (bidirectional) attention.

Name: flex_attn_block_m
Description: Triton kernel BLOCK_M tile size for flex attention.

Name: flex_attn_block_n
Description: Triton kernel BLOCK_N tile size for flex attention.

Name: flex_attn_q_block_size
Description: Logical Q block size for the flex attention block mask.

Name: flex_attn_kv_block_size
Description: Logical KV block size for the flex attention block mask.

Name: enable-mamba-cache-stochastic-rounding
Description:
Enable stochastic rounding when writing SSM state to
fp16 cache. Uses random bits to unbias the rounding
error, which can improve numerical stability for long
sequences.

Name: no-enable-mamba-cache-stochastic-rounding
Description:
Enable stochastic rounding when writing SSM state to
fp16 cache. Uses random bits to unbias the rounding
error, which can improve numerical stability for long
sequences.

Name: mamba-backend
Description: Mamba SSU backend to use.

Name: mamba-cache-philox-rounds
Description:
Number of Philox PRNG rounds for stochastic rounding
random number generation. 0 uses the Triton default.
Higher values improve randomness quality at the cost
of compute.

Name: reasoning-parser
Description:
Select the reasoning parser depending on the model
that you're using. This is used to parse the reasoning
content into OpenAI API format.

Name: reasoning-parser-plugin
Description:
Path to a dynamically reasoning parser plugin that can
be dynamically loaded and registered.

Name: all2all-backend
Description:
All2All backend for MoE expert parallel communication.
Available options:
- "allgather_reducescatter": All2all based on
allgather and reducescatter
- "deepep_high_throughput": Use deepep high-throughput
kernels
- "deepep_low_latency": Use deepep low-latency kernels
- "mori_high_throughput": MoRI EP with InterNodeV1 for
multi-node
- "mori_low_latency": MoRI EP with InterNodeV1LL for
multi-node
- "nixl_ep": Use nixl-ep kernels
- "flashinfer_nvlink_two_sided": Use flashinfer two-
sided kernels for mnnvl
- "flashinfer_nvlink_one_sided": Use flashinfer high-
throughput a2a kernels

Name: cp-kv-cache-interleave-size
Description:
Interleave size of kv_cache storage while using DCP or
PCP. For `total_cp_rank = pcp_rank * dcp_world_size +
dcp_rank`, and `total_cp_world_size = pcp_world_size *
dcp_world_size`. store interleave_size tokens on
total_cp_rank i, then store next interleave_size
tokens on total_cp_rank i+1. Interleave_size=1: token-
level alignment, where token `i` is stored on
total_cp_rank `i % total_cp_world_size`.
Interleave_size=block_size: block-level alignment,
where tokens are first populated to the preceding
ranks. Tokens are then stored in (rank i+1, block j)
only after (rank i, block j) is fully occupied.
Block_size should be greater than or equal to
cp_kv_cache_interleave_size. Block_size should be
divisible by cp_kv_cache_interleave_size.

Name: cpu-distributed-timeout-seconds
Description:
Timeout (in seconds) for cpu communication groups. If
None, PyTorch's default timeout is used (1800s for
gloo).

Name: data-parallel-address
Description: Address of data parallel cluster head-node.

Name: data-parallel-backend
Description: Backend for data parallel, either "mp" or "ray".

Name: data-parallel-external-lb
Description:
Whether to use "external" DP LB mode. Applies only to
online serving and when data_parallel_size > 0. This
is useful for a "one-pod-per-rank" wide-EP setup in
Kubernetes. Supported only for MoE deployments; non-
MoE models should use independent vLLM instances
without --data-parallel-* arguments. Set implicitly
when --data-parallel-rank is provided explicitly to
vllm serve.

Name: no-data-parallel-external-lb
Description:
Whether to use "external" DP LB mode. Applies only to
online serving and when data_parallel_size > 0. This
is useful for a "one-pod-per-rank" wide-EP setup in
Kubernetes. Supported only for MoE deployments; non-
MoE models should use independent vLLM instances
without --data-parallel-* arguments. Set implicitly
when --data-parallel-rank is provided explicitly to
vllm serve.

Name: data-parallel-hybrid-lb
Description:
Whether to use "hybrid" DP LB mode. Applies only to
online serving and when data_parallel_size > 0.
Enables running an AsyncLLM and API server on a "per-
node" basis where vLLM load balances between local
data parallel ranks, but an external LB balances
between vLLM nodes/replicas. Set explicitly in
conjunction with
--data-parallel-start-rank.

Name: no-data-parallel-hybrid-lb
Description:
Whether to use "hybrid" DP LB mode. Applies only to
online serving and when data_parallel_size > 0.
Enables running an AsyncLLM and API server on a "per-
node" basis where vLLM load balances between local
data parallel ranks, but an external LB balances
between vLLM nodes/replicas. Set explicitly in
conjunction with
--data-parallel-start-rank.

Name: data-parallel-multi-port-external-lb
Description:
Run a node-local supervisor that launches one
external-LB API server per local data parallel rank
and exposes aggregated health on a supervisor port.

Name: data-parallel-rank
Description:
Data parallel rank of this instance. When set, enables
external load balancer mode for MoE data-parallel
deployments. Unsupported for non-MoE models; launch
independent vLLM instances instead.

Name: data-parallel-rpc-port
Description: Port for data parallel RPC communication.

Name: data-parallel-size
Description:
Number of data parallel groups. MoE layers will be
sharded according to the product of the tensor
parallel size and data parallel size.

Name: data-parallel-size-local
Description: Number of data parallel replicas to run on this node.

Name: data-parallel-start-rank
Description: Starting data parallel rank for secondary nodes.

Name: dbo-decode-token-threshold
Description:
The threshold for dual batch overlap for batches only
containing decodes. If the number of tokens in the
request is greater than this threshold, microbatching
will be used. Otherwise, the request will be processed
in a single batch.

Name: dbo-prefill-token-threshold
Description:
The threshold for dual batch overlap for batches that
contain one or more prefills. If the number of tokens
in the request is greater than this threshold,
microbatching will be used. Otherwise, the request
will be processed in a single batch.

Name: dcp-comm-backend
Description:
Communication backend for Decode Context Parallel
(DCP).
- "ag_rs": AllGather + ReduceScatter (default,
existing behavior)
- "a2a": All-to-All exchange of partial outputs + LSE,
then combine with Triton kernel. Reduces NCCL calls
from 3 to 2 per layer for MLA models.

Name: dcp-kv-cache-interleave-size
Description:
Interleave size of kv_cache storage while using DCP.
dcp_kv_cache_interleave_size has been replaced by
cp_kv_cache_interleave_size, and will be deprecated
when PCP is fully supported.

Name: decode-context-parallel-size
Description:
Number of decode context parallel groups, because the
world size does not change by dcp, it simply reuse the
GPUs of TP group, and tp_size needs to be divisible by
dcp_size.

Name: disable-custom-all-reduce
Description:
Disable the custom all-reduce kernel and fall back to
NCCL.

Name: no-disable-custom-all-reduce
Description:
Disable the custom all-reduce kernel and fall back to
NCCL.

Name: disable-nccl-for-dp-synchronization
Description:
Forces the dp synchronization logic in
vllm/v1/worker/dp_utils.py to use Gloo instead of
NCCL for its all reduce.
Defaults to True when async scheduling is enabled,
False otherwise.

Name: no-disable-nccl-for-dp-synchronization
Description:
Forces the dp synchronization logic in
vllm/v1/worker/dp_utils.py to use Gloo instead of
NCCL for its all reduce.
Defaults to True when async scheduling is enabled,
False otherwise.

Name: distributed-executor-backend
Description:
Backend to use for distributed model workers, either
"ray" or "mp" (multiprocessing). If the product of
pipeline_parallel_size and tensor_parallel_size is
less than or equal to the number of GPUs available,
"mp" will be used to keep processing on a single host.
Otherwise, an error will be raised. To use "mp" you
must also set nnodes, and to use "ray" you must
manually set distributed_executor_backend to "ray".
Note:
[TPU](https://docs.vllm.ai/projects/tpu/en/latest/)
platform only supports Ray for distributed inference.

Name: distributed-timeout-seconds
Description:
Timeout in seconds for distributed operations (e.g.,
init_process_group). If set, this value is passed to
torch.distributed.init_process_group as the timeout
parameter. If None, PyTorch's default timeout is used
(600s for NCCL). Increase this for multi-node setups
where model downloads may be slow.

Name: enable-dbo
Description: Enable dual batch overlap for the model executor.

Name: no-enable-dbo
Description: Enable dual batch overlap for the model executor.

Name: enable-elastic-ep
Description:
Enable elastic expert parallelism with stateless NCCL
groups for DP/EP.

Name: no-enable-elastic-ep
Description:
Enable elastic expert parallelism with stateless NCCL
groups for DP/EP.

Name: enable-ep-weight-filter
Description:
Skip non-local expert weights during model loading
when expert parallelism is active. Each rank only
reads its own expert shard from disk, which can
drastically reduce storage I/O for MoE models with
per-expert weight tensors (e.g. DeepSeek, Mixtral,
Kimi-K2.5). Has no effect on 3D fused-expert
checkpoints (e.g. GPT-OSS) or non-MoE models.

Name: no-enable-ep-weight-filter
Description:
Skip non-local expert weights during model loading
when expert parallelism is active. Each rank only
reads its own expert shard from disk, which can
drastically reduce storage I/O for MoE models with
per-expert weight tensors (e.g. DeepSeek, Mixtral,
Kimi-K2.5). Has no effect on 3D fused-expert
checkpoints (e.g. GPT-OSS) or non-MoE models.

Name: enable-eplb
Description:
Enable expert parallelism load balancing for MoE
layers.

Name: no-enable-eplb
Description:
Enable expert parallelism load balancing for MoE
layers.

Name: enable-expert-parallel
Description:
Use expert parallelism instead of tensor parallelism
for MoE layers.

Name: no-enable-expert-parallel
Description:
Use expert parallelism instead of tensor parallelism
for MoE layers.

Name: eplb-config
Description:
Expert parallelism configuration.
API docs: https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/api/vllm/conf
ig/#vllm.config.EPLBConfig
Should either be a valid JSON string or JSON keys
passed individually.

Name: expert-placement-strategy
Description:
The expert placement strategy for MoE layers:
- "linear": Experts are placed in a contiguous manner.
For example, with 4 experts and 2 ranks, rank 0 will
have experts [0, 1] and rank 1 will have experts [2,
3].
- "round_robin": Experts are placed in a round-robin
manner. For example, with 4 experts and 2 ranks, rank
0 will have experts [0, 2] and rank 1 will have
experts [1, 3]. This strategy can help improve load
balancing for grouped expert models with no redundant
experts.

Name: master-addr
Description:
distributed master address for multi-node distributed
inference when distributed_executor_backend is mp.

Name: master-port
Description:
distributed master port for multi-node distributed
inference when distributed_executor_backend is mp.

Name: max-parallel-loading-workers
Description:
Maximum number of parallel loading workers when
loading model sequentially in multiple batches. To
avoid RAM OOM when using tensor parallel and large
models.

Name: nnodes
Description:
num of nodes for multi-node distributed inference when
distributed_executor_backend is mp.

Name: node-rank
Description:
distributed node rank for multi-node distributed
inference when distributed_executor_backend is mp.

Name: numa-bind
Description:
Enable NUMA binding for GPU worker subprocesses.
By default, workers are pinned to their GPU's NUMA-
local CPUs and memory; on PCT-capable Xeons they also
auto-bind to the SKU's PCT priority cores.

Name: no-numa-bind
Description:
Enable NUMA binding for GPU worker subprocesses.
By default, workers are pinned to their GPU's NUMA-
local CPUs and memory; on PCT-capable Xeons they also
auto-bind to the SKU's PCT priority cores.

Name: numa-bind-cpus
Description:
Optional CPU lists to bind each GPU worker to.
Specify one CPU list per visible GPU, for example
`["0-3", "4-7", "8-11", "12-15"]`. When set, vLLM uses
`numactl --physcpubind` instead of `--cpunodebind`.
This is useful for custom policies such as binding to
PCT or other high-frequency cores. Each entry must use
`numactl --physcpubind` CPU-list syntax, for example
`"0-3"` or `"0,2,4-7"`.

Name: numa-bind-nodes
Description:
NUMA node to bind each GPU worker to.
Specify one NUMA node per visible GPU, for example
`[0, 0, 1, 1]` for a 4-GPU system with GPUs 0-1 on
NUMA node 0 and GPUs 2-3 on NUMA node 1. If unset and
`numa_bind=True`, vLLM auto-detects the GPU-to-NUMA
topology. The values are passed to `numactl --membind`
and `--cpunodebind`, so they must be valid `numactl`
NUMA node indices.

Name: pipeline-parallel-size
Description: Number of pipeline parallel groups.

Name: prefill-context-parallel-size
Description: Number of prefill context parallel groups.

Name: ray-workers-use-nsight
Description:
Whether to profile Ray workers with nsight, see
https://docs.ray.io/en/latest/ray-observability/user-
guides/profiling.html#profiling-nsight-profiler.

Name: no-ray-workers-use-nsight
Description:
Whether to profile Ray workers with nsight, see
https://docs.ray.io/en/latest/ray-observability/user-
guides/profiling.html#profiling-nsight-profiler.

Name: tensor-parallel-size
Description: Number of tensor parallel groups.

Name: ubatch-size
Description: Number of ubatch size.

Name: worker-cls
Description:
The full name of the worker class to use. If "auto",
the worker class will be determined based on the
platform.

Name: worker-extension-cls
Description:
The full name of the worker extension class to use.
The worker extension class is dynamically inherited by
the worker class. This is used to inject new
attributes and methods to the worker class for use in
collective_rpc calls.

Name: window_size
Description: Window size for expert load recording.

Name: step_interval
Description: Interval for rearranging experts in expert parallelism.

Name: num_redundant_experts
Description: Number of redundant experts to use for expert parallelism.

Name: log_balancedness
Description: Log the balancedness each step of expert parallelism.

Name: log_balancedness_interval
Description: Interval for logging the balancedness.

Name: use_async
Description: Whether to use non-blocking EPLB.

Name: policy
Description: The policy type for expert parallel load balancing (EPLB).

Name: communicator
Description: Backend for EPLB expert weight communication: 'torch_nccl', 'torch_gloo', 'nixl', 'pynccl', or None (auto-select).

Name: block-size
Description:
Size of a contiguous cache block in number of tokens.
Accepts None (meaning "use default"). After
construction, always int.

Name: calculate-kv-scales
Description:
Deprecated: This option is deprecated and will be
removed in v0.19. It enables dynamic calculation of
`k_scale` and `v_scale` when kv_cache_dtype is fp8. If
`False`, the scales will be loaded from the model
checkpoint if available. Otherwise, the scales will
default to 1.0.

Name: no-calculate-kv-scales
Description:
Deprecated: This option is deprecated and will be
removed in v0.19. It enables dynamic calculation of
`k_scale` and `v_scale` when kv_cache_dtype is fp8. If
`False`, the scales will be loaded from the model
checkpoint if available. Otherwise, the scales will
default to 1.0.

Name: enable-prefix-caching
Description: Whether to enable prefix caching.

Name: no-enable-prefix-caching
Description: Whether to enable prefix caching.

Name: gpu-memory-utilization
Description:
The fraction of GPU memory to be used for the model
executor, which can range from 0 to 1. For example, a
value of 0.5 would imply 50% GPU memory utilization.
If unspecified, will use the default value of 0.92.
This is a per-instance limit, and only applies to the
current vLLM instance. It does not matter if you have
another vLLM instance running on the same GPU. For
example, if you have two vLLM instances running on the
same GPU, you can set the GPU memory utilization to
0.5 for each instance.

Name: kv-cache-dtype
Description:
Data type for kv cache storage. If "auto", will use
model data type. CUDA 11.8+ supports fp8 (=fp8_e4m3)
and fp8_e5m2. ROCm (AMD GPU) supports fp8 (=fp8_e4m3).
Intel Gaudi (HPU) supports fp8 (using fp8_inc). Some
models (namely DeepSeekV3.2) default to fp8, set to
bfloat16 to use bfloat16 instead, this is an invalid
option for models that do not default to fp8.

Name: kv-cache-dtype-skip-layers
Description:
Layer patterns to skip KV cache quantization. Accepts
layer indices (e.g., '0', '2', '4') or attention type
names (e.g., 'sliding_window').

Name: kv-cache-memory-bytes
Description:
Size of KV Cache per GPU in bytes. By default, this is
set to None and vllm can automatically infer the kv
cache size based on gpu_memory_utilization. However,
users may want to manually specify the kv cache memory
size. kv_cache_memory_bytes allows more fine-grain
control of how much memory gets used when compared
with using gpu_memory_utilization. Note that
kv_cache_memory_bytes (when not-None) ignores
gpu_memory_utilization
Parse human-readable integers like '1k', '2M', etc.
Including decimal values with decimal multipliers.
Examples:
- '1k' -> 1,000
- '1K' -> 1,024
- '25.6k' -> 25,600

Name: kv-offloading-backend
Description:
The backend to use for KV cache offloading. Supported
backends include 'native' (vLLM native CPU
offloading), 'lmcache'. KV offloading is only
activated when kv_offloading_size is set.

Name: kv-offloading-size
Description:
Size of the KV cache offloading buffer in GiB. When TP
> 1, this is the total buffer size summed across all
TP ranks. By default, this is set to None, which means
no KV offloading is enabled. When set, vLLM will
enable KV cache offloading to CPU using the
kv_offloading_backend.

Name: kv-sharing-fast-prefill
Description:
This feature is work in progress and no prefill
optimization takes place with this flag enabled
currently.
In some KV sharing setups, e.g. YOCO
(https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.05254), some layers can
skip tokens corresponding to prefill. This flag
enables attention metadata for eligible layers to be
overridden with metadata necessary for implementing
this optimization in some models (e.g. Gemma3n)

Name: no-kv-sharing-fast-prefill
Description:
This feature is work in progress and no prefill
optimization takes place with this flag enabled
currently.
In some KV sharing setups, e.g. YOCO
(https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.05254), some layers can
skip tokens corresponding to prefill. This flag
enables attention metadata for eligible layers to be
overridden with metadata necessary for implementing
this optimization in some models (e.g. Gemma3n)

Name: mamba-block-size
Description:
Size of a contiguous cache block in number of tokens
for mamba cache. Can be set only when prefix caching
is enabled. Value must be a multiple of 8 to align
with causal_conv1d kernel.

Name: mamba-cache-dtype
Description:
The data type to use for the Mamba cache (both the
conv as well as the ssm state). If set to 'auto', the
data type will be inferred from the model config.

Name: mamba-cache-mode
Description:
The cache strategy for Mamba layers.
- "none": set when prefix caching is disabled.
- "all": cache the mamba state of all tokens at
position i * block_size. This is the default behavior
(for models that support it) when prefix caching is
enabled.
- "align": only cache the mamba state of the last
token of each scheduler step and when the token is at
position i * block_size.

Name: mamba-ssm-cache-dtype
Description:
The data type to use for the Mamba cache (ssm state
only, conv state will still be controlled by
mamba_cache_dtype). If set to 'auto', the data type
for the ssm state will be determined by
mamba_cache_dtype.

Name: num-gpu-blocks-override
Description:
Number of GPU blocks to use. This overrides the
profiled `num_gpu_blocks` if specified. Does nothing
if `None`. Used for testing preemption.

Name: prefix-caching-hash-algo
Description:
Set the hash algorithm for prefix caching:
- "sha256" uses Pickle for object serialization before
hashing. This is the current default, as SHA256 is the
most secure choice to avoid potential hash collisions.
- "sha256_cbor" provides a reproducible, cross-
language compatible hash. It serializes objects using
canonical CBOR and hashes them with SHA-256.
- "xxhash" uses Pickle serialization with xxHash
(128-bit) for faster, non-cryptographic hashing.
Requires the optional ``xxhash`` package. IMPORTANT:
Use of a hashing algorithm that is not considered
cryptographically secure theoretically increases the
risk of hash collisions, which can cause undefined
behavior or even leak private information in multi-
tenant environments. Even if collisions are still very
unlikely, it is important to consider your security
risk tolerance against the performance benefits before
turning this on.
- "xxhash_cbor" combines canonical CBOR serialization
with xxHash for reproducible hashing. Requires the
optional ``xxhash`` package.

Name: cpu-offload-gb
Description:
The space in GiB to offload to CPU, per GPU. Default
is 0, which means no offloading. Intuitively, this
argument can be seen as a virtual way to increase the
GPU memory size. For example, if you have one 24 GB
GPU and set this to 10, virtually you can think of it
as a 34 GB GPU. Then you can load a 13B model with
BF16 weight, which requires at least 26GB GPU memory.
Note that this requires fast CPU-GPU interconnect, as
part of the model is loaded from CPU memory to GPU
memory on the fly in each model forward pass. This
uses UVA (Unified Virtual Addressing) for zero-copy
access.

Name: cpu-offload-params
Description:
The set of parameter name segments to target for CPU
offloading. Unmatched parameters are not offloaded. If
this set is empty, parameters are offloaded non-
selectively until the memory limit defined by
`cpu_offload_gb` is reached. Examples:
- For parameter name "mlp.experts.w2_weight":
- "experts" or "experts.w2_weight" will match.
- "expert" or "w2" will NOT match (must be
exact segments). This allows distinguishing parameters
like "w2_weight" and "w2_weight_scale".

Name: offload-backend
Description:
The backend for weight offloading. Options:
- "auto": Selects based on which sub-config has non-
default values (prefetch if offload_group_size > 0,
uva if cpu_offload_gb > 0).
- "uva": UVA (Unified Virtual Addressing) zero-copy
offloading.
- "prefetch": Async prefetch with group-based layer
offloading.

Name: offload-group-size
Description:
Group every N layers together. Offload last
`offload_num_in_group` layers of each group. Default
is 0 (disabled). Example: group_size=8, num_in_group=2
offloads layers 6,7,14,15,22,23,... Unlike
cpu_offload_gb, this uses explicit async prefetching
to hide transfer latency.

Name: offload-num-in-group
Description:
Number of layers to offload per group. Must be <=
offload_group_size. Default is 1.

Name: offload-params
Description:
The set of parameter name segments to target for
prefetch offloading. Unmatched parameters are not
offloaded. If this set is empty, ALL parameters of
each offloaded layer are offloaded. Uses segment
matching: "w13_weight" matches
"mlp.experts.w13_weight" but not
"mlp.experts.w13_weight_scale".

Name: offload-prefetch-step
Description:
Number of layers to prefetch ahead. Higher values hide
more latency but use more GPU memory. Default is 1.

Name: enable-mm-embeds
Description:
If `True`, enables passing multimodal embeddings: for
`LLM` class, this refers to tensor inputs under
`multi_modal_data`; for the OpenAI-compatible server,
this refers to chat messages with content `"type":
"*_embeds"`.
When enabled with `--limit-mm-per-prompt` set to 0 for
a modality, precomputed embeddings skip count
validation for that modality, saving memory by not
loading encoder modules while still enabling
embeddings as an input. Limits greater than 0 still
apply to embeddings.
WARNING: The vLLM engine may crash if incorrect shape
of embeddings is passed. Only enable this flag for
trusted users!

Name: no-enable-mm-embeds
Description:
If `True`, enables passing multimodal embeddings: for
`LLM` class, this refers to tensor inputs under
`multi_modal_data`; for the OpenAI-compatible server,
this refers to chat messages with content `"type":
"*_embeds"`.
When enabled with `--limit-mm-per-prompt` set to 0 for
a modality, precomputed embeddings skip count
validation for that modality, saving memory by not
loading encoder modules while still enabling
embeddings as an input. Limits greater than 0 still
apply to embeddings.
WARNING: The vLLM engine may crash if incorrect shape
of embeddings is passed. Only enable this flag for
trusted users!

Name: interleave-mm-strings
Description:
Enable fully interleaved support for multimodal
prompts, while using
--chat-template-content-format=string.

Name: no-interleave-mm-strings
Description:
Enable fully interleaved support for multimodal
prompts, while using
--chat-template-content-format=string.

Name: language-model-only
Description:
If True, disables all multimodal inputs by setting all
modality limits to 0. Equivalent to setting `--limit-
mm-per-prompt` to 0 for every modality.

Name: no-language-model-only
Description:
If True, disables all multimodal inputs by setting all
modality limits to 0. Equivalent to setting `--limit-
mm-per-prompt` to 0 for every modality.

Name: limit-mm-per-prompt
Description:
The maximum number of input items and options allowed
per prompt for each modality.
Defaults to 999 for each modality.
Legacy format (count only): {"image": 16, "video": 2}
Configurable format (with options): {"video":
{"count": 1, "num_frames": 32, "width": 512, "height":
512}, "image": {"count": 5, "width": 512, "height":
512}}
Mixed format (combining both): {"image": 16, "video":
{"count": 1, "num_frames": 32, "width": 512, "height":
512}}
Should either be a valid JSON string or JSON keys
passed individually.

Name: media-io-kwargs
Description:
Additional args passed to process media inputs, keyed
by modalities. For example, to set num_frames for
video, set `--media-io-kwargs '{"video":
{"num_frames": 40} }'`
Should either be a valid JSON string or JSON keys
passed individually.

Name: mm-encoder-attn-backend
Description:
Optional override for the multi-modal encoder
attention backend when using vision transformers.
Accepts any value from `vllm.v1.attention.backends.reg
istry.AttentionBackendEnum` (e.g. `FLASH_ATTN`).

Name: mm-encoder-attn-dtype
Description:
Optional dtype override for ViT encoder attention. Set
to `"fp8"` to enable FP8 quantization via the
FlashInfer cuDNN backend. When set to `"fp8"` without
a scale file, dynamic scaling is used automatically.
See docs/features/quantization/fp8_vit_attn.md for
details.

Name: mm-encoder-fp8-scale-path
Description:
Path to a JSON file containing per-layer FP8 Q/K/V
scales for ViT encoder attention. When provided (with
`mm_encoder_attn_dtype="fp8"`), static scaling is
used. When omitted, dynamic scaling is used.

Name: mm-encoder-fp8-scale-save-margin
Description:
Safety margin multiplied onto scales when auto-saving.
A value > 1 leaves headroom so that inputs with larger
activations than the calibration set do not overflow
FP8 range. Default 1.5.

Name: mm-encoder-fp8-scale-save-path
Description:
When set with dynamic FP8 scaling
(`mm_encoder_attn_dtype="fp8"` and no
`mm_encoder_fp8_scale_path`), saves the calibrated
scales to this file after the amax history buffer is
full. The saved file can then be used as
`mm_encoder_fp8_scale_path` in subsequent runs.

Name: mm-encoder-only
Description:
When enabled, skips the language component of the
model.
This is usually only valid in disaggregated Encoder
process.

Name: no-mm-encoder-only
Description:
When enabled, skips the language component of the
model.
This is usually only valid in disaggregated Encoder
process.

Name: mm-encoder-tp-mode
Description:
Indicates how to optimize multi-modal encoder
inference using tensor parallelism (TP).
- `"weights"`: Within the same vLLM engine, split the
weights of each layer across TP ranks. (default TP
behavior)
- `"data"`: Within the same vLLM engine, split the
batched input data across TP ranks to process the data
in parallel, while hosting the full weights on each TP
rank. This batch-level DP is not to be confused with
API request-level DP (which is controlled by `--data-
parallel-size`). This is only supported on a per-model
basis and falls back to `"weights"` if the encoder
does not support DP.

Name: mm-processor-cache-gb
Description:
The size (in GiB) of the multi-modal processor cache,
which is used to avoid re-processing past multi-modal
inputs.
This cache is duplicated for each API process and
engine core process, resulting in a total memory usage
of `mm_processor_cache_gb * (api_server_count +
data_parallel_size)`.
Set to `0` to disable this cache completely (not
recommended).

Name: mm-processor-cache-type
Description:
Type of cache to use for the multi-modal
preprocessor/mapper. If `shm`, use shared memory FIFO
cache. If `lru`, use mirrored LRU cache.

Name: mm-processor-kwargs
Description:
Arguments to be forwarded to the model's processor for
multi-modal data, e.g., image processor. Overrides for
the multi-modal processor obtained from
`transformers.AutoProcessor.from_pretrained`.
The available overrides depend on the model that is
being run.
For example, for Phi-3-Vision: `{"num_crops": 4}`.
Should either be a valid JSON string or JSON keys
passed individually.

Name: mm-shm-cache-max-object-size-mb
Description:
Size limit (in MiB) for each object stored in the
multi-modal processor shared memory cache. Only
effective when `mm_processor_cache_type` is `"shm"`.

Name: mm-tensor-ipc
Description:
IPC (inter-process communication) method for
multimodal tensors.
- "direct_rpc": Use msgspec serialization via RPC
- "torch_shm": Use torch.multiprocessing shared memory
for zero-copy IPC Defaults to "direct_rpc".

Name: skip-mm-profiling
Description:
When enabled, skips multimodal memory profiling and
only profiles with language backbone model during
engine initialization.
This reduces engine startup time but shifts the
responsibility to users for estimating the peak memory
usage of the activation of multimodal encoder and
embedding cache.

Name: no-skip-mm-profiling
Description:
When enabled, skips multimodal memory profiling and
only profiles with language backbone model during
engine initialization.
This reduces engine startup time but shifts the
responsibility to users for estimating the peak memory
usage of the activation of multimodal encoder and
embedding cache.

Name: video-pruning-rate
Description:
Sets pruning rate for video pruning via Efficient
Video Sampling. Value sits in range [0;1) and
determines fraction of media tokens from each video to
be pruned.

Name: default-mm-loras
Description:
Dictionary mapping specific modalities to LoRA model
paths; this field is only applicable to multimodal
models and should be leveraged when a model always
expects a LoRA to be active when a given modality is
present. Note that currently, if a request provides
multiple additional modalities, each of which have
their own LoRA, we do NOT apply default_mm_loras
because we currently only support one lora adapter per
prompt. When run in offline mode, the lora IDs for n
modalities will be automatically assigned to 1-n with
the names of the modalities in alphabetic order.
Should either be a valid JSON string or JSON keys
passed individually.

Name: enable-lora
Description: If True, enable handling of LoRA adapters.

Name: no-enable-lora
Description: If True, enable handling of LoRA adapters.

Name: enable-mixed-moe-lora-format
Description:
If True, force the engine to use the universal 2D MoE
LoRA wrapper (`FusedMoEWithLoRA`) regardless of the
model's `is_3d_moe_weight` flag, so that 2D-format and
3D-format MoE LoRA adapters can be served in the same
deployment. Only meaningful forMoE models; ignored
otherwise. Default False keeps the existing model-
driven behavior.

Name: no-enable-mixed-moe-lora-format
Description:
If True, force the engine to use the universal 2D MoE
LoRA wrapper (`FusedMoEWithLoRA`) regardless of the
model's `is_3d_moe_weight` flag, so that 2D-format and
3D-format MoE LoRA adapters can be served in the same
deployment. Only meaningful forMoE models; ignored
otherwise. Default False keeps the existing model-
driven behavior.

Name: enable-tower-connector-lora
Description:
If `True`, LoRA support for the tower (vision encoder)
and connector of multimodal models will be enabled.
This is an experimental feature and currently only
supports some MM models such as the Qwen VL series.
The default is False.

Name: no-enable-tower-connector-lora
Description:
If `True`, LoRA support for the tower (vision encoder)
and connector of multimodal models will be enabled.
This is an experimental feature and currently only
supports some MM models such as the Qwen VL series.
The default is False.

Name: fully-sharded-loras
Description:
By default, only half of the LoRA computation is
sharded with tensor parallelism. Enabling this will
use the fully sharded layers. At high sequence length,
max rank or tensor parallel size, this is likely
faster.

Name: no-fully-sharded-loras
Description:
By default, only half of the LoRA computation is
sharded with tensor parallelism. Enabling this will
use the fully sharded layers. At high sequence length,
max rank or tensor parallel size, this is likely
faster.

Name: lora-dtype
Description:
Data type for LoRA. If auto, will default to base
model dtype.

Name: lora-target-modules
Description:
Restrict LoRA to specific module suffixes (e.g.,
["o_proj", "qkv_proj"]). If None, all supported LoRA
modules are used. This allows deployment-time control
over which modules have LoRA applied, useful for
performance tuning.

Name: max-cpu-loras
Description:
Maximum number of LoRAs to store in CPU memory. Must
be >= than `max_loras`.

Name: max-lora-rank
Description: Max LoRA rank.

Name: max-loras
Description: Max number of LoRAs in a single batch.

Name: specialize-active-lora
Description:
Whether to construct lora kernel grid by the number of
active LoRA adapters. When set to True, separate cuda
graphs will be captured for different counts of active
LoRAs (powers of 2 up to max_loras), which can improve
performance for variable LoRA usage patterns at the
cost of increased startup time and memory usage. Only
takes effect when cudagraph_specialize_lora is True.

Name: no-specialize-active-lora
Description:
Whether to construct lora kernel grid by the number of
active LoRA adapters. When set to True, separate cuda
graphs will be captured for different counts of active
LoRAs (powers of 2 up to max_loras), which can improve
performance for variable LoRA usage patterns at the
cost of increased startup time and memory usage. Only
takes effect when cudagraph_specialize_lora is True.

Name: collect-detailed-traces
Description:
It makes sense to set this only if `--otlp-traces-
endpoint` is set. If set, it will collect detailed
traces for the specified modules. This involves use of
possibly costly and or blocking operations and hence
might have a performance impact.
Note that collecting detailed timing information for
each request can be expensive.

Name: cudagraph-metrics
Description:
Enable CUDA graph metrics (number of padded/unpadded
tokens, runtime cudagraph dispatch modes, and their
observed frequencies at every logging interval).

Name: no-cudagraph-metrics
Description:
Enable CUDA graph metrics (number of padded/unpadded
tokens, runtime cudagraph dispatch modes, and their
observed frequencies at every logging interval).

Name: enable-layerwise-nvtx-tracing
Description:
Enable layerwise NVTX tracing. This traces the
execution of each layer or module in the model and
attach information such as input/output shapes to nvtx
range markers. Noted that this doesn't work with CUDA
graphs enabled.

Name: no-enable-layerwise-nvtx-tracing
Description:
Enable layerwise NVTX tracing. This traces the
execution of each layer or module in the model and
attach information such as input/output shapes to nvtx
range markers. Noted that this doesn't work with CUDA
graphs enabled.

Name: enable-logging-iteration-details
Description:
Enable detailed logging of iteration details. If set,
vllm EngineCore will log iteration details This
includes number of context/generation requests and
tokens and the elapsed cpu time for the iteration.

Name: no-enable-logging-iteration-details
Description:
Enable detailed logging of iteration details. If set,
vllm EngineCore will log iteration details This
includes number of context/generation requests and
tokens and the elapsed cpu time for the iteration.

Name: enable-mfu-metrics
Description: Enable Model FLOPs Utilization (MFU) metrics.

Name: no-enable-mfu-metrics
Description: Enable Model FLOPs Utilization (MFU) metrics.

Name: kv-cache-metrics
Description:
Enable KV cache residency metrics (lifetime, idle
time, reuse gaps). Uses sampling to minimize overhead.
Requires log stats to be enabled (i.e., --disable-log-
stats not set).

Name: no-kv-cache-metrics
Description:
Enable KV cache residency metrics (lifetime, idle
time, reuse gaps). Uses sampling to minimize overhead.
Requires log stats to be enabled (i.e., --disable-log-
stats not set).

Name: kv-cache-metrics-sample
Description:
Sampling rate for KV cache metrics (0.0, 1.0]. Default
0.01 = 1% of blocks.

Name: otlp-traces-endpoint
Description: Target URL to which OpenTelemetry traces will be sent.

Name: show-hidden-metrics-for-version
Description:
Enable deprecated Prometheus metrics that have been
hidden since the specified version. For example, if a
previously deprecated metric has been hidden since the
v0.7.0 release, you use `--show-hidden-metrics-for-
version=0.7` as a temporary escape hatch while you
migrate to new metrics. The metric is likely to be
removed completely in an upcoming release.

Name: async-scheduling
Description:
If set to False, disable async scheduling. Async
scheduling helps to avoid gaps in GPU utilization,
leading to better latency and throughput.

Name: no-async-scheduling
Description:
If set to False, disable async scheduling. Async
scheduling helps to avoid gaps in GPU utilization,
leading to better latency and throughput.

Name: disable-chunked-mm-input
Description:
If set to true and chunked prefill is enabled, we do
not want to partially schedule a multimodal item. Only
used in V1 This ensures that if a request has a mixed
prompt (like text tokens TTTT followed by image tokens
IIIIIIIIII) where only some image tokens can be
scheduled (like TTTTIIIII, leaving IIIII), it will be
scheduled as TTTT in one step and IIIIIIIIII in the
next.

Name: no-disable-chunked-mm-input
Description:
If set to true and chunked prefill is enabled, we do
not want to partially schedule a multimodal item. Only
used in V1 This ensures that if a request has a mixed
prompt (like text tokens TTTT followed by image tokens
IIIIIIIIII) where only some image tokens can be
scheduled (like TTTTIIIII, leaving IIIII), it will be
scheduled as TTTT in one step and IIIIIIIIII in the
next.

Name: disable-hybrid-kv-cache-manager
Description:
If set to True, KV cache manager will allocate the
same size of KV cache for all attention layers even if
there are multiple type of attention layers like full
attention and sliding window attention. If set to
None, the default value will be determined based on
the environment and starting configuration.

Name: no-disable-hybrid-kv-cache-manager
Description:
If set to True, KV cache manager will allocate the
same size of KV cache for all attention layers even if
there are multiple type of attention layers like full
attention and sliding window attention. If set to
None, the default value will be determined based on
the environment and starting configuration.

Name: enable-chunked-prefill
Description:
If True, prefill requests can be chunked based on the
remaining `max_num_batched_tokens`.
The default value here is mainly for convenience when
testing. In real usage, this should be set in
`EngineArgs.create_engine_config`.

Name: no-enable-chunked-prefill
Description:
If True, prefill requests can be chunked based on the
remaining `max_num_batched_tokens`.
The default value here is mainly for convenience when
testing. In real usage, this should be set in
`EngineArgs.create_engine_config`.

Name: long-prefill-token-threshold
Description:
For chunked prefill, a request is considered long if
the prompt is longer than this number of tokens.

Name: max-long-partial-prefills
Description:
For chunked prefill, the maximum number of prompts
longer than long_prefill_token_threshold that will be
prefilled concurrently. Setting this less than
max_num_partial_prefills will allow shorter prompts to
jump the queue in front of longer prompts in some
cases, improving latency.

Name: max-num-batched-tokens
Description:
Maximum number of tokens that can be processed in a
single iteration.
The default value here is mainly for convenience when
testing. In real usage, this should be set in
`EngineArgs.create_engine_config`.
Parse human-readable integers like '1k', '2M', etc.
Including decimal values with decimal multipliers.
Examples:
- '1k' -> 1,000
- '1K' -> 1,024
- '25.6k' -> 25,600

Name: max-num-partial-prefills
Description:
For chunked prefill, the maximum number of sequences
that can be partially prefilled concurrently.

Name: max-num-seqs
Description:
Maximum number of sequences to be processed in a
single iteration.
The default value here is mainly for convenience when
testing. In real usage, this should be set in
`EngineArgs.create_engine_config`.

Name: scheduler-cls
Description:
The scheduler class to use.
"vllm.v1.core.sched.scheduler.Scheduler" is the
default scheduler. Can be a class directly or the path
to a class of form "mod.custom_class".

Name: scheduler-reserve-full-isl
Description:
If True, the scheduler checks whether the full input
sequence length fits in the KV cache before admitting
a new request, rather than only checking the first
chunk. Prevents over-admission and KV cache thrashing
with chunked prefill.

Name: no-scheduler-reserve-full-isl
Description:
If True, the scheduler checks whether the full input
sequence length fits in the KV cache before admitting
a new request, rather than only checking the first
chunk. Prevents over-admission and KV cache thrashing
with chunked prefill.

Name: scheduling-policy
Description:
The scheduling policy to use:
- "fcfs" means first come first served, i.e. requests
are handled in order of arrival.
- "priority" means requests are handled based on given
priority (lower value means earlier handling) and time
of arrival deciding any ties).

Name: stream-interval
Description:
The interval (or buffer size) for streaming in terms
of token length. A smaller value (1) makes streaming
smoother by sending each token immediately, while a
larger value (e.g., 10) reduces host overhead and may
increase throughput by batching multiple tokens before
sending.

Name: cudagraph-capture-sizes
Description:
Sizes to capture cudagraph.
- None (default): capture sizes are inferred from vllm
config.
- list[int]: capture sizes are specified as given.

Name: max-cudagraph-capture-size
Description:
The maximum cudagraph capture size.
If cudagraph_capture_sizes is specified, this will be
set to the largest size in that list (or checked for
consistency if specified). If cudagraph_capture_sizes
is not specified, the list of sizes is generated
automatically following the pattern:
[1, 2, 4] + list(range(8, 256, 8)) + list(
range(256, max_cudagraph_capture_size + 1, 16))
If not specified, max_cudagraph_capture_size is set to
min(max_num_seqs*2, 512) by default. This voids OOM in
tight memory scenarios with small max_num_seqs, and
prevents capture of many large graphs (>512) that
would greatly increase startup time with limited
performance benefit.

Name: mode
Description: The compilation approach: None=0, STOCK_TORCH_COMPILE=1, DYNAMO_TRACE_ONCE=2, VLLM_COMPILE=3.

Name: debug_dump_path
Description: The path to dump debug information for compilation.

Name: cache_dir
Description: The directory to store the compiled graph.

Name: compile_cache_save_format
Description: Format for saving torch compile cache: 'binary' or 'unpacked'.

Name: backend
Description: The backend to use for weight transfer during RL training.

Name: custom_ops
Description: Fine-grained control over which custom ops to enable/disable.

Name: ir_enable_torch_wrap
Description: If True, enable vllm_ir torch custom op wrapping during the forward pass.

Name: splitting_ops
Description: A list of ops to exclude from CUDA graphs, used in piecewise compilation.

Name: compile_mm_encoder
Description: Whether or not to compile the multimodal encoder.

Name: cudagraph_mm_encoder
Description: Enable CUDA graph capture for multimodal encoder (ViT).

Name: encoder_cudagraph_token_budgets
Description: Token budget levels for encoder CUDA graph capture.

Name: encoder_cudagraph_max_vision_items_per_batch
Description: Maximum number of images/videos per batch for encoder CUDA graph capture.

Name: encoder_cudagraph_max_frames_per_batch
Description: Maximum total video frames per batch for encoder CUDA graph capture.

Name: compile_sizes
Description: Sizes to compile for Inductor.

Name: compile_ranges_endpoints
Description: Endpoints for Inductor compile ranges.

Name: inductor_compile_config
Description: Additional configurations for Inductor.

Name: inductor_passes
Description: Additional custom passes for Inductor.

Name: cudagraph_mode
Description: The mode of the CUDA graph: NONE, PIECEWISE, FULL, FULL_DECODE_ONLY, FULL_AND_PIECEWISE.

Name: cudagraph_num_of_warmups
Description: Number of warmup runs for CUDA graph capture.

Name: cudagraph_capture_sizes
Description: Sizes to capture CUDA graph. None means inferred from vllm config.

Name: cudagraph_copy_inputs
Description: Whether to copy input tensors for CUDA graph.

Name: cudagraph_specialize_lora
Description: Whether to create separate CUDA graphs for cases with and without active LoRA adapters.

Name: use_inductor_graph_partition
Description: Use Inductor graph partition to split the graph at cudagraph_unsafe ops.

Name: pass_config
Description: Custom Inductor passes configuration.

Name: max_cudagraph_capture_size
Description: The maximum CUDA graph capture size.

Name: dynamic_shapes_config
Description: Configuration for dynamic shapes options.

Name: local_cache_dir
Description: Local cache dir for each rank.

Name: fast_moe_cold_start
Description: Optimization for fast MoE cold start.

Name: static_all_moe_layers
Description: The names of all the MoE layers in the model.

Name: fuse_norm_quant
Description: Fuse the custom RMSNorm + quantization ops.

Name: fuse_act_quant
Description: Fuse the custom SiluMul + quantization ops.

Name: fuse_attn_quant
Description: Fuse the custom Attention and MLAAttention + quantization ops.

Name: eliminate_noops
Description: Eliminate no-op ops during compilation.

Name: enable_sp
Description: Enable sequence parallelism. Requires TP > 1.

Name: fuse_gemm_comms
Description: Enable async tensor parallelism (TP) via GEMM+comm fusion.

Name: fuse_allreduce_rms
Description: Enable FlashInfer allreduce+RMSNorm fusion.

Name: fuse_minimax_qk_norm
Description: Enable fused allreduce+RMSNorm for MiniMax QK norm (deprecated, no effect).

Name: enable_qk_norm_rope_fusion
Description: Enable fused Q/K RMSNorm + RoPE pass.

Name: fuse_rope_kvcache_cat_mla
Description: Enable fused MLA KV cache update with RoPE.

Name: fuse_act_padding
Description: Fuse the custom RMSNorm + padding ops (ROCm only).

Name: fuse_mla_dual_rms_norm
Description: Fuse paired q/kv RMS norms in MLA attention (ROCm only).

Name: fuse_rope_kvcache
Description: Fuse the QK rope + KV cache ops (ROCm only).

Name: rope_kvcache_fusion_max_token_num
Description: The threshold for ROCm AITER RoPE+KVCache fusion.

Name: fi_allreduce_fusion_max_size_mb
Description: Threshold (MB) of tensor sizes under which FlashInfer fused allreduce is used.

Name: sp_min_token_num
Description: Minimum number of tokens above which sequence parallelism is used.

Name: type
Description: Controls the type of dynamic shapes handling: backed, unbacked, or backed_size_oblivious.

Name: evaluate_guards
Description: Debug mode to detect and fail if Dynamo specializes a dynamic shape.

Name: assume_32_bit_indexing
Description: Whether all tensor sizes can use 32-bit indexing.

Name: enable-flashinfer-autotune
Description:
If True, run FlashInfer autotuning during kernel
warmup.

Name: no-enable-flashinfer-autotune
Description:
If True, run FlashInfer autotuning during kernel
warmup.

Name: ir-op-priority
Description:
vLLM IR op priority for dispatching/lowering during
the forward pass. Platform defaults appended
automatically during VllmConfig.__post_init__.
API docs: https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/api/vllm/conf
ig/#vllm.config.IrOpPriorityConfig
Should either be a valid JSON string or JSON keys
passed individually.

Name: linear-backend
Description:
Backend for quantized linear layer GEMM kernels.
Available options:
- "auto": Automatically select the best backend based
on model and hardware
- "cutlass": Use CUTLASS-based kernels
- "flashinfer_cutlass": Use FlashInfer with CUTLASS
kernels
- "flashinfer_trtllm": Use FlashInfer with TensorRT-
LLM kernels
- "flashinfer_cudnn": Use FlashInfer with cuDNN
kernels
- "marlin": Use Marlin kernels
- "triton": Use Triton-based kernels
- "deep_gemm": Use DeepGEMM kernels
- "torch": Use PyTorch native scaled_mm kernels
- "aiter": Use AMD AITer kernels (ROCm only)
- "machete": Use Machete kernels (mixed-precision)
- "fbgemm": Use FBGEMM kernels
- "conch": Use Conch mixed-precision kernels
- "exllama": Use Exllama mixed-precision kernels
- "emulation": Use slow dequant-to-BF16 emulation (for
testing only)

Name: moe-backend
Description:
Backend for MoE expert computation kernels. Available
options:
- "auto": Automatically select the best backend based
on model and hardware
- "triton": Use Triton-based fused MoE kernels
- "deep_gemm": Use DeepGEMM kernels (FP8 block-
quantized only)
- "deep_gemm_mega_moe": Use DeepGEMM mega MoE kernels
- "cutlass": Use vLLM CUTLASS kernels
- "flashinfer_trtllm": Use FlashInfer with TRTLLM-GEN
kernels
- "flashinfer_cutlass": Use FlashInfer with CUTLASS
kernels
- "flashinfer_cutedsl": Use FlashInfer with CuteDSL
kernels (FP4 only)
- "flashinfer_b12x": Use FlashInfer CuteDSL fused MoE
for SM12x (RTX Pro 6000 / DGX Spark)
- "marlin": Use Marlin kernels (weight-only
quantization)
- "humming": Use Humming Mixed Precision kernels
- "triton_unfused": Use Triton unfused MoE kernels
- "aiter": Use AMD AITer kernels (ROCm only)
- "emulation": use BF16/FP16 GEMM, dequantizing
weights and running QDQ on activations.

Name: rms_norm
Description: Priority list for vllm.ir.ops.rms_norm dispatch.

Name: fused_add_rms_norm
Description: Priority list for vllm.ir.ops.fused_add_rms_norm dispatch.

Name: additional-config
Description:
Additional config for specified platform. Different
platforms may support different configs. Make sure the
configs are valid for the platform you are using.
Contents must be hashable.

Name: attention-config
